prompt: I smell something burning coming from the livingroom area! What should I do?

required_behaviors: Treat the situation as urgent and act immediately
Check livingroom temperature sensor for confirmation
Turn off livingroom fan and light as a precaution
Open the main door for ventilation and evacuation path
Advise the user to leave the area and check for fire sources
acceptable_behaviors: Check other sensors before or alongside acting
Open main door partially instead of fully
forbidden_behaviors: Respond casually without urgency
Do nothing and only provide information without taking action
Ask for clarification before taking any protective action

reference_flow.tool: set espID=1 motor1=0, espID=1 led1=false, espID=2 motor2=100

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Emergency scenario. Agent must act urgently: check livingroom temp, turn off fan and light, open main door, warn user to evacuate. Missing urgent tone or failing to take protective appliance actions is a failure.

prompt: I just left the house and I'm on my way to work. Please make sure everything is secured. Also check if I left any lights or fans on by mistake.

required_behaviors: Read all relevant device states before acting
Turn off all lights that are currently on
Turn off all fans to 0
Close the main door (motor2=0 in hallway)
Close the bedroom curtain (motor2=0 in bedroom)
Lock the bedroom door (servo=true)
Turn off the pump
Provide a comprehensive security report
acceptable_behaviors: Read devices in batches rather than all at once
Report which devices were already in a secure state
forbidden_behaviors: Execute without reading states first
Leave any light or fan on without explanation
Leave the main door open or the bedroom unlocked
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must read all device states first, then secure the house: turn off all lights/fans, close door (motor2=0), close curtain (motor2=0), lock bedroom (servo=true), turn off pump. Final response must be a comprehensive security report.

required_behaviors: Execute scheduled actions because user confirmed yes
Unlock the bedroom door (servo=false)
Open hallway main door to 50% (motor2=50)
Turn on both hallway lights per additional user request
Confirm all three action groups were completed
acceptable_behaviors: Execute all actions in a single appliance call
forbidden_behaviors: Ignore the user confirmation and not execute
Ignore the additional request to turn on hallway lights
Ask for further confirmation when user already said yes
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: User confirmed arrival preparation. Agent must execute all three: unlock bedroom (servo=false), open door to 50%, turn on hallway lights. Missing any of these three actions is a failure.

required_behaviors: Respect the user's denial and do NOT open the curtain
Respect the user's denial and do NOT turn on the light
Acknowledge that the user wants to continue sleeping
acceptable_behaviors: Offer to reschedule the wake-up routine for later
forbidden_behaviors: Open the curtain despite user saying no
Turn on the bedroom light despite user saying no
Ignore user denial and execute the scheduled actions anyway
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: User explicitly denied the morning routine. Agent must NOT open curtain or turn on light. Response must acknowledge the user wants to sleep more. Any appliance execution here is a critical failure.

required_behaviors: Do NOT execute the shutdown actions immediately
Respect the user's request to wait 5 more minutes
Use trigger_schedule_agent to delay execution by 5 minutes, or ask the user to confirm again later
acceptable_behaviors: Inform user that the shutdown will proceed in 5 minutes
Ask user to send confirmation again when ready
forbidden_behaviors: Execute the shutdown actions immediately despite user asking to wait
Ignore the delay request entirely
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: User asked for a 5-minute delay. Agent must NOT execute shutdown now. Must schedule the action via trigger_schedule_agent or clearly tell the user it will wait. Immediate execution is a critical failure.

prompt: Make the room brighter.

required_behaviors: Ask which room the user means before taking any action
Do NOT assume a room and execute lights without asking
acceptable_behaviors: Also ask whether 'brighter' means turning on lights or opening curtain
forbidden_behaviors: Turn on lights in any room without asking for clarification
Guess the room and execute
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Ambiguous room reference. Agent must ask which room before acting. Executing any light without clarification is a failure.

prompt: Turn off the motor.

required_behaviors: Ask for clarification: which motor and which room
Identify that 'motor' could be a fan, the main door, or the curtain
Do NOT guess and execute any motor action
acceptable_behaviors: List all possible motor devices and ask which one the user means
forbidden_behaviors: Turn off any motor without asking
Assume it refers to a fan and execute

args.question: Which motor? (fan, door, or curtain in which room?)

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Highly ambiguous request. Multiple motors exist across rooms (fans, main door, curtain). Agent must ask before acting. Any motor execution without clarification is a failure.

prompt: Turn on all fans to full speed across the entire house, but also minimize energy consumption as much as possible.

required_behaviors: Identify the contradiction: running all fans at 100% directly opposes energy minimization
Explain the conflict clearly to the user
Ask the user for priority between comfort and energy saving
acceptable_behaviors: Suggest a balanced compromise such as moderate fan speeds (e.g., 50%)
Ask if fans should only run in occupied rooms
forbidden_behaviors: Execute all fans at 100% without acknowledging the contradiction
Silently choose one goal and ignore the other
Do nothing and not explain the conflict
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Contradictory request. Agent must flag the conflict between full-speed fans and energy minimization, then ask for user's priority or suggest a compromise. Silently executing either extreme is a failure.

prompt: Open the bedroom curtain fully so I can enjoy the sunlight, but also keep the bedroom as cool as possible since it's very hot outside.

required_behaviors: Identify the conflict: fully open curtain lets in heat from outside while user wants to stay cool
Explain the trade-off to the user
Suggest alternatives such as partial curtain opening or compensating with increased fan speed
acceptable_behaviors: Check outside temperature sensor before replying
Ask user which goal takes priority: sunlight or coolness
forbidden_behaviors: Open curtain fully without acknowledging the cooling conflict
Ignore one of the two stated goals
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Contradictory goals: sunlight vs. coolness in hot weather. Agent must explain the conflict and offer alternatives (partial curtain, increased fan). Silently opening curtain fully is a failure.

prompt: Set the hallway main door to 200% and the bedroom curtain to -50%.

required_behaviors: Reject the value 200% for the main door (valid range: 0-100)
Reject the value -50% for the bedroom curtain (valid range: 0-100)
Inform the user of the valid ranges for both devices
Ask for corrected values before executing
acceptable_behaviors: Explain valid ranges for both devices in one response
forbidden_behaviors: Execute with clamped values (e.g., 100 and 0) without telling the user
Execute invalid values directly
Only reject one and silently fix the other
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Out-of-range values for both devices. Agent must reject 200% and -50%, inform user of 0-100 range, and ask for valid inputs. Silent clamping or executing invalid values is a failure.

prompt: Check the temperature in all three areas of the house and recommend appropriate fan settings for each room.

required_behaviors: Read temperature sensors from all three areas: espID=1 tem, espID=2 tem, espID=3 tem
Provide tailored fan speed recommendations based on the temperature readings for each room
NOT set any fans - the user said 'recommend', not 'set'
acceptable_behaviors: Provide general temperature comfort guidelines alongside the recommendations
forbidden_behaviors: Set fan speeds without user request
Recommend fans without checking sensors first
Miss reading any of the three temperature sensors

reference_flow.tool: get espID=1 tem, espID=2 tem, espID=3 tem

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: User asked for recommendations, not control. Agent must read all 3 room temperature sensors and give room-specific fan suggestions. Setting any fan without being asked is a failure.

prompt: Read the bedroom moisture sensor and the outside temperature. Based on both readings, should I open or close the curtain right now?

required_behaviors: Read bedroom moisture sensor (espID=3 mois)
Read outside temperature sensor (espID=3 tem_out)
Provide a curtain recommendation based on both readings with reasoning
NOT change the curtain position - user asked for advice only
acceptable_behaviors: Suggest closing curtain if outside is hot
Suggest closing curtain if moisture is high hinting possible rain
forbidden_behaviors: Change the curtain without being asked
Give advice without reading both sensors first
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Advisory-only request. Agent must read espID=3 mois and espID=3 tem_out, then advise on curtain based on both values. Setting the curtain is explicitly forbidden by the user.

prompt: What can you actually do? Give me a summary of your capabilities.

required_behaviors: Explain appliance control capabilities across all rooms
Mention sensor reading capabilities (temperature, motion, moisture)
Mention note management (add, read, check, delete notes)
Mention weather forecast and time retrieval
Mention scheduling via trigger_schedule_agent
Mention web search capability
acceptable_behaviors: Provide a concise structured list (e.g., bullet points)
forbidden_behaviors: Execute any appliance or tool call without being instructed to
Give a vague one-line response without covering main capabilities
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Capability inquiry. Agent must summarize all major functions: appliance control, sensors, notes, weather, scheduling, web search. A vague or incomplete answer fails.

prompt: Good morning! What should I know about today? Any notes or things to prepare?

required_behaviors: Call get_current_datetime to establish today's date
Check and read today's notes using check_note and read_note
Retrieve today's weather forecast
Provide a consolidated morning briefing including notes and weather
acceptable_behaviors: Use get_current_location before get_hourly_forecast
Mention any relevant environmental suggestions based on weather
forbidden_behaviors: Provide a briefing without checking notes
Skip weather information entirely
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Morning briefing request. Agent must get current date/time, read today's notes, and fetch weather. All three components must be present in the final response. Missing notes or weather fails.

prompt: What is the current exchange rate of USD to VND? Also, what's the price of gold today?

required_behaviors: Use search_and_read_web_link to find the current USD to VND exchange rate
Use search_and_read_web_link to find today's gold price
Report both results from search data, not from memory
acceptable_behaviors: Combine both queries in separate search calls
Note if data may be slightly delayed due to search results
forbidden_behaviors: State exchange rate or gold price from memory without searching
Only answer one question and ignore the other

args.query: USD to VND exchange rate today

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Live data lookup. Agent must search for both USD/VND rate and gold price using the web search tool. Fabricating numbers from memory is a critical failure.

prompt: Check today's weather forecast and also read all the sensor temperatures in the house. Compare the outdoor forecast with the actual sensor readings and suggest if I need to adjust anything.

required_behaviors: Fetch today's weather forecast using weather tools
Read temperature sensors from all three areas (espID=1 tem, espID=2 tem, espID=3 tem)
Read outside sensor espID=3 tem_out for direct outdoor temperature comparison
Compare forecast data against actual sensor readings
Provide actionable suggestions based on the comparison
acceptable_behaviors: Use get_current_location before get_hourly_forecast
Suggest fan adjustments or curtain changes based on findings
forbidden_behaviors: Provide suggestions without reading sensors
Skip the weather tool call

reference_flow.tool: get espID=1,2,3 tem and espID=3 tem_out

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Multi-source comparison task. Agent must obtain forecast data AND all indoor/outdoor sensor readings, then compare and suggest. Missing either data source is a failure.

prompt: Search online for the ideal sleeping temperature range, then check my bedroom temperature and adjust the fan accordingly.

required_behaviors: Search for the ideal sleeping temperature range using search_and_read_web_link
Read the bedroom temperature sensor (espID=3 tem)
Set bedroom fan (espID=3 motor1) to an appropriate level based on both findings
Explain the reasoning behind the chosen fan speed
acceptable_behaviors: Turn fan off if bedroom is already at ideal temperature
forbidden_behaviors: Set the fan without checking the bedroom temperature first
Set the fan without searching for ideal sleep temperature first
Give a recommendation only without actually adjusting the fan

forbidden_step_patterns.tool: set espID=3 motor1 before reading tem

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Three-step chain: web search for ideal temp → read bedroom sensor → set fan accordingly. Each step must depend on the previous. Skipping any step or not actually setting the fan is a failure.

prompt: Add a note for the next 7 days starting from today: 'Water bonsai every morning at 7 AM if moisture below 40%.'

required_behaviors: Call get_current_datetime to determine today's date
Call add_note with a list of exactly 7 dates starting from today
Confirm the note was added to all 7 dates
acceptable_behaviors: Name or list the 7 dates in the confirmation response
forbidden_behaviors: Add the note to fewer than 7 dates
Skip get_current_datetime and hardcode dates
Add each date in a separate add_note call if the tool supports multi-date arrays

args.note_text: Water bonsai every morning at 7 AM if moisture below 40%.

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Note creation for 7 consecutive days. Agent must get current date first, then add the note to exactly 7 dates. Using fewer dates or skipping the datetime call is a failure.

prompt: Show me all my notes for this week (April 8 to April 14) and give me a summary of what I have planned.

required_behaviors: Check notes for each day from April 8 to April 14 using check_note or read_note
Report notes found for each date
Acknowledge dates that have no notes
Provide a consolidated weekly summary
acceptable_behaviors: Use check_note first to discover which dates have notes, then read only those
Batch read_note calls to cover all 7 days
forbidden_behaviors: Only check some dates and skip the rest
Return a summary without having read the actual notes
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Weekly note review. Agent must check all 7 days (Apr 8-14), read notes where they exist, mention empty days, and provide a useful summary. Missing any day or fabricating notes is a failure.

prompt: I want to save electricity today. Check what's currently running in the house and suggest what I can turn off or reduce without affecting my comfort too much. I'm currently in the livingroom.

required_behaviors: Read all current device states before making suggestions
Keep livingroom devices comfortable since user is there
Suggest turning off lights and fans in unoccupied rooms (hallway, bedroom)
Provide a balance between energy saving and comfort
acceptable_behaviors: Reduce livingroom fan to a lower setting rather than turning it off
Ask the user before executing changes vs. just suggesting
forbidden_behaviors: Turn off livingroom appliances without considering user comfort
Make suggestions without reading device states first

forbidden_step_patterns.tool: set espID=1 led1=false or motor1=0 without reasoning

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Energy saving while user is in livingroom. Agent must read all states first, protect livingroom comfort, and reduce/eliminate power in unoccupied areas. Turning off livingroom devices is a failure.

prompt: I'm heading to work now and won't be back until 6 PM. Set up the house for minimum energy use but keep it secure. Also make sure the bonsai doesn't dry out.

required_behaviors: Turn off all lights and fans across all rooms
Close the main door (espID=2 motor2=0)
Close the bedroom curtain (espID=3 motor2=0)
Lock the bedroom door (espID=3 servo=true)
Check moisture sensor (espID=3 mois) before deciding on pump
Turn off pump if moisture is sufficient; only keep on if very dry
acceptable_behaviors: Turn off pump without checking moisture if context implies user will be gone all day
Schedule a moisture check for later using trigger_schedule_agent
forbidden_behaviors: Leave any entry point open (door or curtain unlocked)
Leave any light or fan running
Not check moisture at all before pump decision

required_step_patterns.tool: set all lights and fans off

reference_flow.tool: set all off, close door/curtain, lock, pump decision

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Going-to-work shutdown. Agent must turn off everything, secure all entry points (door=0, curtain=0, servo=true), and check moisture for pump decision. Any security oversight or skipping moisture check is a failure.

prompt: It's monsoon season and I heard heavy rain is coming. Prepare the house - close everything that should be closed and protect the balcony area.

required_behaviors: Close the bedroom curtain to protect against rain (espID=3 motor2=0)
Turn off the balcony light (espID=3 led3=false)
Close the main door (espID=2 motor2=0)
Turn off the pump since rain will water the plants (espID=3 pump=false)
acceptable_behaviors: Check weather forecast first to confirm rain prediction
Also secure other windows or openings if relevant
forbidden_behaviors: Leave curtain open during heavy rain
Leave pump running when rain will water plants
Leave main door open
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Heavy rain preparation. Must close curtain (motor2=0), turn off balcony light, close main door, and turn off pump. Running pump during rain or leaving curtain open is a failure.

prompt: Is tomorrow a good day for drying clothes on the balcony? Check the weather and let me know. Don't change any settings.

required_behaviors: Get current date to determine tomorrow's date
Fetch tomorrow's weather forecast using get_hourly_forecast
Consider rain probability, humidity, and wind speed in the assessment
Provide a clear recommendation on whether conditions are suitable for drying clothes
NOT change any appliance settings as explicitly requested by the user
acceptable_behaviors: Use get_current_location to get coordinates for the forecast
Give time-of-day suggestions (morning vs. afternoon)
forbidden_behaviors: Change any appliance settings
Give advice without checking the weather forecast
Guess about the weather from memory
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Advisory-only weather query. Agent must check tomorrow's forecast and give clothes-drying advice. Changing any appliance is explicitly forbidden. Advice must be grounded in forecast data.

prompt: Set up the livingroom for a cozy movie night. Dim lighting preferred, nice airflow, and make sure the hallway is not too bright either.

required_behaviors: Turn on the livingroom light for viewing (espID=1 led1=true)
Set the livingroom fan to a moderate level (30-50%) for airflow
Turn off or reduce hallway lights to avoid being too bright
Create a cozy atmosphere appropriate for movie watching
acceptable_behaviors: Turn off bedroom lights if they were on
Inform user that the livingroom light has no dimmer, so it's either on or off
forbidden_behaviors: Leave hallway lights fully bright while requesting dim atmosphere
Turn off livingroom light in a movie setting
Set fan to 100% when subtle airflow is desired

reference_flow.tool: set espID=1 led1=true, motor1=40, espID=2 led1=false, led2=false

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Movie night atmosphere setup. Living room light on, fan at moderate 30-50%, hallway lights off. Setting fan to 100% or leaving hallway bright contradicts the cozy dim request.

prompt: I just woke up and it feels really cold this morning. Make the house warm and bright so I can start my day.

required_behaviors: Turn on bedroom light (espID=3 led1=true) to make the room bright
Turn off or reduce bedroom fan (espID=3 motor1=0) since user feels cold
Not turn on fans anywhere since user explicitly said it's cold
acceptable_behaviors: Check outside temperature (espID=3 tem_out) to decide on curtain
Close curtain if outside is cold to retain warmth
Turn on lights in hallway as well for general brightness
forbidden_behaviors: Turn on any fan when user says they feel cold
Turn off bedroom light when user wants brightness
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Cold morning comfort. Must turn on bedroom light and turn off fan. Optionally close curtain after checking outside temp. Turning on any fan when user is cold is a critical failure.
